seoTitle: How to Transfer a Domain to Cloudflare Registrar
metaDescription: Move a domain from any registrar to Cloudflare Registrar: unlock it, get your EPP code, import DNS records, and complete the transfer.
intro: Transferring a domain to Cloudflare Registrar moves the actual registration, not just the DNS, which means it's a slightly different process from a nameserver switch. This guide covers unlocking your domain at the old registrar, getting your transfer code, and completing the move.

steps.heading: Unlock your domain and disable DNSSEC
steps.body: At your current registrar, unlock the domain for transfer and turn off DNSSEC to avoid conflicts.

steps.heading: Get your EPP authorization code
steps.body: Request your unique EPP transfer code from your old provider and copy it.

steps.heading: Add the domain to Cloudflare
steps.body: Log into Cloudflare, click 'Add a Site', and let the automated system scan and import your DNS records.

steps.heading: Start the transfer
steps.body: Navigate to the Domain Registration transfer hub and paste in your EPP authorization code, verifying your registrant contact info.

steps.heading: Confirm payment and approve
steps.body: Submit your at-cost renewal payment, then approve the release email from your old registrar to speed up the transfer window.

faq.question: What is an EPP code and why do I need it?
faq.answer: The EPP (Extensible Provisioning Protocol) code is a unique authorization string from your current registrar that proves you own the domain and authorizes the transfer.

faq.question: How long does a domain transfer to Cloudflare take?
faq.answer: Domain transfers typically take about five to seven days, though approving the release email at your old registrar can help speed up the process.

faq.question: Will my website go down during the transfer?
faq.answer: If your DNS records are correctly imported into Cloudflare beforehand, your site and email should keep functioning normally throughout the transfer.

faq.question: Does Cloudflare charge a markup for domain transfers?
faq.answer: No, Cloudflare processes transfers at an at-cost renewal rate without adding a markup, consistent with its wholesale registrar pricing model.
